example: Losing that job was a blessing in disguise; it pushed me to start my own business.
Stop beating around the bush and tell me what you really think.
I think I'll skip the party; I'm feeling under the weather.

idiom: hit the nail on the head
example: You hit the nail on the head with that analysis.

idiom: let the cat out of the bag
example: He let the cat out of the bag about the surprise party.

idiom: kill two birds with one stone
example: Running errands on my way to work kills two birds with one stone.
We only see each other once in a blue moon.
Who spilled the beans about our plan?
Relax, I'm just pulling your leg.
Break a leg on your audition!

meaning: учиться или работать до глубокой ночи
example: She burned the midnight oil to meet the deadline.
He got cold feet before the wedding.
I decided to bite the bullet and pay the fine.

idiom: the ball is in your court
example: I've done all I can; the ball is in your court.
I'm exhausted; time to hit the sack.

meaning: делать быстро и некачественно, чтобы сэкономить усилия
example: Don't cut corners on this project; quality matters.
Let's make sure we're on the same page before we start.
Cheap souvenirs are a dime a dozen here.
It's late; let's call it a day.
He finally faced the music for his mistakes.
